join_order.notes: custkeys dense 1..1500000, bitset ~187KB fits in cache
use lineitem_orderkey_index for contiguous range lookups per qualifying order

data_structures.aggregation: NONE — o_orderkey is PK so each qualifying order produces exactly one complete group. No hash table needed. Compute revenue sum inline from lineitem rows and feed directly into per-thread top-10 heap.
data_structures.topk: per-thread fixed array of 10 ResultRow structs (240 bytes per thread, stack-allocated). Each thread maintains a min-heap of its best 10 results. After all threads finish, merge 64×10=640 candidates into global top-10.

parallelism.notes: CRITICAL: Do NOT allocate per-thread hash tables. Each thread needs only a stack-allocated top-10 array (240 bytes). Thread-local state is trivial — no heap allocation, no memset of large buffers. Use a simple lambda capturing a local ResultRow[10] array.

indexes.purpose: contiguous range lookup for lineitem rows per qualifying orderkey

optimization_notes: Category A — Plan restructuring (eliminate unnecessary data structure). Root cause: 77ms of the 107ms total was spent outside measured phases, in allocating and zeroing 64 thread-local hash tables (64 × 32K slots × 24 bytes = 48MB total via new + memset). These hash tables are completely unnecessary because o_orderkey is a primary key — each qualifying order produces exactly one aggregation group with a complete revenue sum computed inline from its lineitem rows. No cross-order aggregation ever occurs. Fix: eliminate all per-thread hash tables. Each thread maintains only a stack-allocated top-10 min-heap (10 × 24 bytes = 240 bytes). For each qualifying order, sum revenue from its lineitem rows via the index, then compare against the thread-local top-10. This eliminates 48MB of allocation/zeroing overhead, removes hash table insert/probe costs, and removes the hash table scan for top-10 extraction. Expected: dim_filter ~4ms + main_scan ~26ms + merge <1ms ≈ ~30ms total.
